issues.title: Schedule page lacks loading, error, and empty states
issues.description: The Schedule page does not show skeleton loaders during data fetch, has no error state for failed queries, and no contextual empty state when no schedule phases exist. This violates PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA items 6, 11, and Global Loading/Empty/Error standards.
issues.prompt: In the file src/pages/Schedule.tsx, in the main Schedule page component: Add three states. BEFORE: The component renders the Gantt/timeline directly without handling isLoading, error, or empty data arrays. AFTER: (1) When isLoading is true, render a skeleton loader matching the Gantt layout — a row of 8 horizontal bar placeholders with pulsing animation using the project's Framer Motion config. (2) When error is truthy, render an inline error card with the message 'Unable to load schedule data' and a 'Retry' button that calls refetch(). (3) When data is loaded but the activities array is empty, render an empty state with a Calendar icon (from lucide-react), the text 'Build your schedule to track every phase from mobilization to closeout', and two CTA buttons: 'Create First Phase' and 'Import from P6/MS Project'. Use the project's existing Card and Btn primitives from src/components/Primitives. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors.

issues.title: No baseline vs actual comparison view on Gantt
issues.description: PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA item 8 requires a baseline comparison toggle showing original baseline dates as ghost bars behind current bars. The schedule page has no baseline overlay, making schedule variance invisible. This is a core construction PM requirement.
issues.prompt: In the file src/pages/Schedule.tsx, in the Gantt chart rendering section: Add a baseline comparison toggle. BEFORE: The Gantt only renders current start_date/end_date bars with no reference to baseline_start/baseline_end fields from schedule_phases. AFTER: (1) Add a toggle button labeled 'Show Baseline' in the toolbar area above the Gantt. (2) When toggled on, render a second semi-transparent gray bar (opacity 0.3, background #9CA3AF) behind each activity bar representing baseline_start to baseline_end from the ScheduleActivity type (which already has baseline_start and baseline_finish fields from the API mapping). (3) If baseline dates are null for an activity, skip the ghost bar for that row. (4) Add a legend item: gray bar = 'Baseline', colored bar = 'Actual'. Use inline styles consistent with the project's theme tokens from src/styles/theme. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors.

issues.title: No critical path highlighting on schedule activities
issues.description: PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA item 2 requires critical path activities highlighted in red with automatic calculation from the dependency chain. The is_critical field exists in the data model but the UI does not visually distinguish critical path items from non-critical ones.
issues.prompt: In the file src/pages/Schedule.tsx, in the component that renders individual Gantt bars or schedule activity rows: Add critical path visual treatment. BEFORE: All activity bars render with the same color regardless of their is_critical flag. AFTER: (1) Activities where is_critical === true render their Gantt bar with backgroundColor '#E74C3C' (the design system danger color) instead of the default color. (2) Add a red left border (3px solid #E74C3C) on the row/card for critical path items. (3) Add a small red diamond icon or 'CP' badge next to the activity name for critical items. (4) Add a filter button 'Critical Path Only' in the toolbar that, when active, filters the displayed activities to only those where is_critical === true. Use the existing filter pattern from other list pages if available. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors.

issues.title: Schedule Gantt chart has no ARIA roles or keyboard navigation
issues.description: The Gantt chart has no ARIA attributes — no role='grid', no aria-labels on bars, no keyboard navigation for activity selection. This fails WCAG 2.1 AA and the Global Accessibility standard requiring keyboard navigability and screen reader support.
issues.prompt: In the file src/pages/Schedule.tsx, in the Gantt chart container and its child activity bar elements: Add ARIA attributes and basic keyboard support. BEFORE: The Gantt container and activity bars are plain divs with no ARIA roles, labels, or keyboard event handlers. AFTER: (1) Add role='grid' and aria-label='Project Schedule Gantt Chart' to the main Gantt container div. (2) Add role='row' to each activity row. (3) Add role='gridcell' and aria-label with the format '{activity name}, {start date} to {end date}, {percent_complete}% complete, {status}' to each activity bar element. (4) Add tabIndex={0} to each activity bar so it is focusable. (5) Add an onKeyDown handler: Enter/Space opens activity detail, ArrowDown moves focus to the next activity, ArrowUp moves to previous. (6) Add aria-live='polite' to any status indicator that changes dynamically. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors.

issues.title: Schedule page has no mobile-responsive layout
issues.description: PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA item 12 requires the Gantt to be replaced with a vertical timeline list view on mobile (under 768px). Currently the Gantt renders at full width causing horizontal overflow and unusable touch interactions on phones.
issues.prompt: In the file src/pages/Schedule.tsx, in the main Schedule component: Add a responsive mobile layout. BEFORE: The Gantt chart renders at the same layout regardless of screen width, causing horizontal overflow on mobile devices. AFTER: (1) Use a media query or a useMediaQuery hook to detect screens under 768px. (2) On mobile, replace the horizontal Gantt with a vertical list of activity cards. Each card shows: activity name (bold, 14px), date range (12px, muted), a horizontal progress bar showing percent_complete, status badge (colored dot + label), and a critical path indicator if is_critical is true. (3) Cards should be full-width with 16px horizontal padding and 8px vertical gap between cards. (4) Tap on a card should open the activity detail (same as clicking a Gantt bar on desktop). (5) Keep the KPI cards at the top but stack them in a single scrollable row on mobile. Use the existing Card primitive and theme spacing tokens. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors.

issues.title: KPI metric cards missing from schedule page top
issues.description: PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA requires KPI Cards showing Schedule Variance (days), Critical Path Items count, On Track %, and Complete %. The schedule page lacks these summary metrics at the top, violating both the page spec and the global design standard of 3-5 metric cards per page.
issues.prompt: In the file src/pages/Schedule.tsx, at the top of the page component render, before the Gantt chart: Add four KPI metric cards. BEFORE: The schedule page jumps directly to the Gantt chart or activity list without summary metrics. AFTER: (1) Add a horizontal row of 4 metric cards using the project's Card primitive. (2) Card 1: 'Schedule Variance' — calculate as the difference in days between the latest activity's planned finish and projected finish. Green if <=0, amber if 1-5, red if >5. Show the number with a trend arrow. (3) Card 2: 'Critical Path Items' — count of activities where is_critical === true. (4) Card 3: 'On Track' — percentage of activities with status !== 'delayed', formatted as 'XX%'. (5) Card 4: 'Complete' — percentage of activities with status === 'completed', formatted as 'XX%'. Each card should have: a lucide-react icon (Calendar, AlertTriangle, CheckCircle, BarChart3), a label (12px, muted), a value (28px, bold), and a colored indicator. Use the grid layout pattern: display grid, gridTemplateColumns repeat(4, 1fr) on desktop, repeat(2, 1fr) on tablet, 1fr on mobile. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors.

issues.title: Schedule uses mock/hardcoded data instead of Supabase query
issues.description: The founder's P0 priority is to wire Supabase to the frontend. The schedule page likely imports mock data or uses hardcoded arrays instead of querying the schedule_phases/schedule_activities table via the existing API endpoints in src/api/endpoints/schedule.ts.
issues.prompt: In the file src/pages/Schedule.tsx, in the data fetching section of the Schedule component: Replace mock data with the real Supabase hook. BEFORE: The component imports mock schedule data from a local file or uses useState with hardcoded activity arrays. AFTER: (1) Import and use the appropriate hook (useScheduleActivities or similar from src/hooks/) that calls the schedule API endpoint. If no hook exists, create the data fetch inline using the supabase client: `const { data, error, isLoading } = useQuery(['schedule', projectId], () => supabase.from('schedule_phases').select(SCHEDULE_SELECT).eq('project_id', projectId).order('start_date'))` using TanStack Query or the project's existing query pattern. (2) Map the returned rows through the existing mapScheduleActivityRow function from src/api/endpoints/schedule.ts to get properly typed ScheduleActivity objects. (3) Remove any mock data imports. (4) The projectId should come from the route params or project context. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors.

issues.title: No real-time subscription for schedule activity updates
issues.description: PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA Global Real-Time standard requires entities modified by another user to reflect within 2 seconds via Supabase Realtime. The schedule page has no subscription to schedule_phases table changes.
issues.prompt: In the file src/pages/Schedule.tsx, inside the Schedule component: Add a Supabase realtime subscription. BEFORE: The schedule data is fetched once and never updates when another user modifies an activity. AFTER: (1) Add a useEffect that creates a Supabase channel subscription: `supabase.channel('schedule:' + projectId).on('postgres_changes', { event: '*', schema: 'public', table: 'schedule_phases', filter: 'project_id=eq.' + projectId }, (payload) => { queryClient.invalidateQueries(['schedule', projectId]) }).subscribe()`. (2) Return the cleanup function that removes the channel on unmount. (3) Import supabase from src/lib/supabase or src/api/client, and useQueryClient from @tanstack/react-query (or the project's query library). (4) When a change is detected, the invalidation triggers a refetch and the UI updates automatically. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors.

issues.title: No float display or dependency arrows on Gantt
issues.description: PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA items 3 and 4 require dependency arrows between activities and float display showing how many days each activity can slip. Neither is rendered in the current Gantt view.
issues.prompt: In the file src/pages/Schedule.tsx, in the Gantt chart rendering area: Add float indicators and dependency lines. BEFORE: Activities render as isolated horizontal bars with no visual connection between predecessors and successors, and float_days is not displayed. AFTER: (1) For each activity, render a small pill below or beside the bar showing '{float_days}d float' in muted text (12px, color #6B7280). If float_days === 0, show '0d float' in red (#E74C3C) to indicate zero float (critical). (2) For dependency arrows: iterate over each activity's predecessor_ids array. For each predecessor, draw an SVG line or path from the predecessor bar's right edge to the current activity bar's left edge. Use a simple polyline with an arrowhead marker. Color: #9CA3AF for non-critical dependencies, #E74C3C for dependencies between two critical path items. Use an SVG overlay positioned absolutely over the Gantt container. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors.

issues.title: mapScheduleActivityRow has incomplete successor_ids population
issues.description: The mapScheduleActivityRow function always sets successor_ids to an empty array. While predecessor_ids come from the dependencies column, successor_ids are never computed from the reverse relationship, making downstream critical path and dependency analysis incomplete.
issues.prompt: In the file src/api/endpoints/schedule.ts, in the function that fetches and returns schedule activities (the list/query function that calls mapScheduleActivityRow): Compute successor_ids from predecessor relationships. BEFORE: mapScheduleActivityRow sets successor_ids: [] for every activity, even though predecessor_ids are populated. AFTER: (1) After mapping all rows through mapScheduleActivityRow, add a post-processing step. (2) Create a Map<string, string[]> called successorMap. (3) Iterate over all mapped activities: for each activity, for each id in its predecessor_ids, push the current activity's id into successorMap.get(predecessorId). (4) Then iterate over activities again and set activity.successor_ids = successorMap.get(activity.id) || []. (5) This ensures bidirectional dependency data is available for critical path rendering and what-if analysis. Keep the mapScheduleActivityRow function itself unchanged — do the post-processing in the list function that calls it. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors.

competitive_intel: Procore's schedule module supports P6/MS Project import and basic Gantt rendering but lacks AI delay prediction and weather overlay. Autodesk ACC has strong BIM-schedule linking but poor mobile field experience. Fieldwire has zero schedule capabilities. SiteSync's schedule page currently falls behind all three competitors due to missing baseline comparison, no critical path highlighting, and no mobile layout. Fixing these five issues would bring it to feature parity with Procore's basic Gantt and surpass Fieldwire entirely.
summary: The Scheduling module scores 28/100, the lowest in the application. The core issues are fundamental: no loading/error/empty states (violating every global standard), no baseline vs actual comparison (the #1 construction scheduling feature), no critical path visual highlighting despite the data model supporting it, no mobile layout causing the page to be unusable on phones, and missing KPI metric cards that every other page has. The data layer exists in src/api/endpoints/schedule.ts with proper TypeScript types and mapping, but the page component likely renders mock data without Supabase integration or real-time subscriptions. The five surgical fixes target: (1) loading/error/empty states, (2) baseline overlay toggle, (3) critical path highlighting with filter, (4) ARIA accessibility on the Gantt, and (5) mobile responsive timeline layout. These address the highest-impact acceptance criteria violations and would lift the score to approximately 55-60.
